title: The Real Threat of Automation
subtitle: 自动化的真实代价：繁荣表象下的就业困境

paragraphs.en: Automation and its role in the economy have become central topics in American public debate. Technology in general, and automation in particular, are dramatically reshaping the way we work. We need a plan for what is still to come.
paragraphs.zh: 自动化及其在经济中的作用，已成为美国公共讨论的核心议题。广义上的技术，尤其是自动化，正在深刻重塑我们的工作方式。对于即将到来的变化，我们必须提前做好准备。

paragraphs.en: We do not have to look far to see automation's destructive effects. From automated warehouses to cashierless grocery stores to neighborhood libraries with self-checkout machines, automation is replacing workers while creating too few good jobs in return.
paragraphs.zh: 我们无需远眺就能看到自动化的破坏性影响。从自动化仓库、无人收银杂货店，到配备自助借还机的社区图书馆，自动化正在取代工人，却未能创造出足够多的优质新岗位作为回报。

paragraphs.en: The figures from manufacturing are especially striking. Despite widespread fears about trade, a recent report found that only 13 percent of manufacturing job losses were caused by trade, while most were the result of technological advances.
paragraphs.zh: 制造业的数据尤其惊人。尽管人们普遍担心贸易问题，但一份最新报告发现，制造业岗位流失中只有13%源于贸易，而绝大部分是技术进步所致。

paragraphs.en: That is why more people are questioning the ever-expanding role of technology in the economy. The United States is producing more manufactured goods than ever, but it is doing so with fewer workers. And the losses are no longer limited to factories.
paragraphs.zh: 这正是越来越多人开始质疑技术在经济中不断扩张作用的原因。美国制造的工业品比以往任何时候都多，但完成这些生产所需的工人却更少了。而且这种岗位流失已不再局限于工厂。

paragraphs.en: Software and information technology are also threatening jobs that many people once assumed were safe in a rapidly changing economy. Something transformative is happening in America, and it is hurting working families.
paragraphs.zh: 软件和信息技术也在威胁着许多原以为能在快速变化经济中保住的工作。美国正在发生某种深刻变革，而这种变革正在伤害工薪家庭。

words.meaning: adj. 变革性的；有改造力的

paragraphs.en: Whether politicians admit it or not, workers have made their anxiety clear: they feel economically insecure and want good jobs to remain in the United States. So why do so many people still ignore the dangers of automation?
paragraphs.zh: 无论政客承认与否，工人们的焦虑已表露无遗：他们缺乏经济安全感，希望好工作能留在美国。那么，为什么仍有那么多人无视自动化的危险？

words.meaning: adj. 无安全感的；不牢靠的

paragraphs.en: At a moment when planning for the future is more important than ever, many leaders refuse to look ahead. Resisting automation may be futile; like industrialization before it, automation is probably inevitable.
paragraphs.zh: 在比以往任何时候都更需要为未来做规划的时刻，许多领导者却拒绝向前看。抵制自动化或许是徒劳的；就像当年的工业化一样，自动化恐怕势不可挡。

paragraphs.en: I sincerely hope those who say automation will make us more efficient and create new occupations are right. But the damage it is already doing to workers gives me reason to doubt them. No one can say with confidence where the new jobs will come from or when they will arrive.
paragraphs.zh: 我真心希望那些声称自动化会提高效率并创造新职业的人是对的。但它已给劳动者带来的伤害，让我有理由怀疑他们。没有人能有把握地说清新工作将从哪里来，又会在何时出现。

paragraphs.en: Any sensible company or country should prepare for multiple possibilities. This is not alarmism: according to economic research, one in six men aged 25 to 54 in the United States is out of work today.
paragraphs.zh: 任何理性的公司或国家，都应该为多种可能性做好准备。这并非危言耸听：根据经济研究，如今美国25至54岁的男性中，每六人就有一人失业。

paragraphs.en: Fifty years ago, nearly all men in that age group had jobs. Meanwhile, women's labor-force participation has fallen back to levels last seen in the late 1980s. American families and many business leaders already know that automation poses a serious problem.
paragraphs.zh: 五十年前，这个年龄段几乎所有男性都有工作。与此同时，女性的劳动参与率已回落至20世纪80年代末的水平。美国家庭和许多商界领袖其实已认识到，自动化是个严峻问题。

paragraphs.en: The value of a college degree and the promise of upward mobility are both fading. If we want an economy in which everyone can achieve real economic security, we need to start thinking seriously about how to respond to automation.
paragraphs.zh: 大学学历的价值和向上流动的承诺都在消退。如果我们希望建立一个所有人都能获得真正经济安全的经济体系，就必须开始认真思考如何应对自动化。
